# Cadre

> Cadre is an AI co-reviewer for parametric CAD, in the browser. You model a part and AI
> flags manufacturability issues — thin walls, steep overhangs, enclosed voids, non-watertight
> solids — grounded in the geometry the kernel actually measures, with concrete parameter fixes.
> It runs in any modern browser with no install and exports to STL, OBJ, STEP, 3MF, and glTF/GLB.

## Documentation
- [Overview](https://docs.cadre3d.com/): what Cadre does, with links to each topic
- [Architecture](https://docs.cadre3d.com/architecture.html): trust properties — one geometry authority, one review pipeline, verified export
- [Methodology](https://docs.cadre3d.com/methodology.html): the claims Cadre makes and how to verify each one yourself
- [Trust and data](https://docs.cadre3d.com/trust.html): no-install, offline, local-first, data ownership, formats
- [Modeling](https://docs.cadre3d.com/features/modeling.html): sketch, primitives, feature tree, booleans, variables
- [AI review](https://docs.cadre3d.com/features/ai-review.html): manufacturability flags grounded in measured geometry
- [Review inbox](https://docs.cadre3d.com/features/review-inbox.html): one queue for AI and human suggestions
- [Collaboration](https://docs.cadre3d.com/features/collaboration.html): presence and asynchronous fork-and-suggest review
- [Export and interop](https://docs.cadre3d.com/features/export-interop.html): formats, scripting surface, review JSON
- [Measurement integrity](https://docs.cadre3d.com/features/measurement-integrity.html): volume, area, and watertightness computed from geometry

## Try it
- [Open Cadre](https://cadre3d.com/): the live app, no install
